assertions.description: PerformanceResourceTiming exposes redirectStart — the field a redirect chain populates and that Timing-Allow-Origin gates for cross-origin callers.

Following a same-origin redirect chain populates redirectStart/redirectEnd on the resource entry (redirectEnd >= redirectStart > 0), which is the timing the cross-origin opt-in unlocks via Timing-Allow-Origin.
assertions.test: (async()=>{const u='/v151/cross-origin-redirect-timing-opt-in/redirect-chain?hops=2&tao=1&_='+Date.now();const r=await fetch(u,{cache:'no-store'});await r.text();for(let i=0;i<20;i++){const es=performance.getEntriesByType('resource').filter(e=>e.name.indexOf('/redirect-chain')>=0);const e=es[es.length-1];if(e&&e.redirectStart>0)return e.redirectEnd>=e.redirectStart;await new Promise(x=>setTimeout(x,100));}return false;})()

assertions.description: The opt-in is the Timing-Allow-Origin response header; a Headers object accepts and preserves it (the mechanism a redirect hop must send to expose its timing cross-origin).
